communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am stating the purpose of this request so that you may assist me as required by California Government Code (“Gov”) § 7922.600(a)(1). The purpose of this California Public Records Act request is to determine the existence or non-existence of a specific record.

Please note that I am not requesting the record itself, only confirmation of its existence or non-existence. Thus, Civil Code § 1798.90.55, law enforcement exemptions, the public interest exemption or any other laws or exemptions do not apply to, and are not relevant to, this request.

Please confirm the existence or non-existence of the following Flock Safety ALPR footage record:

License Plate: WCD6448 Texas
Date: March 23, 2026
Time Frame: 11:50 AM - 11:59 AM
Camera Location: Monterey Ave at Capitola Ave
Camera Lat/long: 36.97288449560181, -121.9504733710856

The following provisions of the CPRA are expected to apply to *all* elements of this request:

A:  Per Article 1, Section 3(b)(2) of the California Constitution, broadly construe all provisions that further the public’s right to access, and apply any limitations on access as narrowly as possible.

B:  Per §7922.535(a), catalogue the responsive records or information, and describe their location. The identification of all records is applicable even if the record is withheld, pursuant to a lawful exemption to the CPRA, or redacted in whole or in part.

C:  Pursuant to this PRA request, and to your obligations under GOV § 7922.600(a), which obligates public agencies to assist the public to make focused and effective requests that reasonably describe identifiable records, with respect to all exempted or withheld documents I request assistance in the following ways:

a. Please assist me in identifying records and information responsive to the request or purpose of the request;

b. Please describe the information technology and physical location in which the records exist;

c. Please provide me with suggestions for overcoming any practical basis for denying access to the records or information sought.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

David Moore

title: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Canidates Forum Altercation

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

RE: Wisconsin Open Records Request — Ames/Hoiland Altercation, March 18, 2026 — Candidates Debate

Dear Chief 

Pursuant to Wisconsin Statute § 19.35(1)(a) and the Wisconsin Public Records Law, I hereby request access to and copies of all records in the possession, custody, or control of the Lake Geneva Police Department relating to an altercation that occurred on March 18, 2026, between Cheri Ames and Joel Hoiland at or in connection with the Lake Geneva aldermanic candidates debate held that evening.

RECORDS REQUESTED

I request all of the following records without limitation as to form or format:

1. Incident and Police Reports
All initial incident reports, supplemental reports, narratives, and the final investigative report and conclusions prepared by any officer involved in responding to or investigating the March 18, 2026 altercation.

2. Video and Audio Recordings
All video recordings, body-worn camera footage, dashcam recordings, surveillance video, and any other audio or visual recordings captured by Lake Geneva Police Department personnel in connection with this incident, including recordings made at or near the debate venue.

3. Witness and Party Statements
All written, recorded, or transcribed statements obtained from any party, witness, or individual in connection with the investigation of this altercation, including but not limited to statements from Cheri Ames, Joel Hoiland, and civilian witness Joe Esposito.

4. Joe Esposito Witness Statement and Related Records
All records relating to the statement provided by civilian witness Joe Esposito, including his written or recorded statement, any follow-up communications between Mr. Esposito and the Department, and any records reflecting how his account was considered in the investigation.

5. Communications
All internal communications, emails, text messages, radio logs, CAD (Computer Aided Dispatch) records, and dispatch audio relating to the incident, including the initial call for service, officer response, and any subsequent departmental communications regarding the matter.

6. Investigative Notes and Materials
All field notes, investigative memoranda, photographs, and any other documents created or collected in the course of investigating this incident.

7. Final Report and Conclusions
The final police department report, findings, and conclusions resulting from the investigation of this altercation, including any supervisory review or approval of those conclusions.

8. Disposition Records
Any records reflecting the outcome of the investigation, including any referrals to the Walworth County District Attorney's office, charging decisions, declination of prosecution, or other final disposition of the matter.

FORMAT AND DELIVERY

I request that records be provided in electronic format (PDF or native digital format) via email. If any records exist only in physical form, I request legible copies. If electronic delivery is not practicable, please advise and I will arrange for alternative delivery.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Gary Milliette

title: AB63 Program Records - Revenue Allocation, Policies, and Performance Data

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

SECTION A: PROGRAM-WIDE RECORDS

All records reflecting the City's internal policies and procedures for the AB63 discovery program, including how estimated gross receipts figures are generated when a taxpayer does not respond, and how cases are assigned, tracked, and resolved.

All records reflecting response time targets, service level agreements, or internal performance metrics for the AB63 Unit and the Office of Finance Customer Service Division.

All records reflecting the total number of AB63 notices issued in 2024 and 2025, broken down by month if available.

All records reflecting the rate at which AB63 assessments are disputed, corrected, or reduced following taxpayer response, for the years 2022 to the present.

All records reflecting complaints filed against the Office of Finance related to incorrect assessments, delayed responses, or interest accrual on disputed or unresolved amounts, for the years 2022 to the present.

All records reflecting communications between the Office of Finance and the California Franchise Tax Board regarding the AB63 program generally, including any data sharing agreements, program guidelines, or correspondence about program scope and implementation.

All records reflecting any audits, reviews, or evaluations of the AB63 program conducted internally or by any outside body, including any findings related to accuracy of assessments or taxpayer response handling.

All records reflecting the average time between the Office of Finance receiving FTB data identifying an unregistered business and the issuance of a discovery notice to that business, for the years 2015 to the present.

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

communications.subject: 2026-IGFP-00563 Pace, Michael Acknowledgement and Notice Certification of Identity Required
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Pace:

This acknowledges receipt of your March 30 Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to the U.S. Postal Service Office of Inspector General (OIG) for records related to:


  *   Missing mail, delivery irregularities, and the handling of prior complaints associated with United States Postal Service operations in Lanesborough, Massachusetts, including mail directed to PO Box 1033 and/or addresses associated with Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort.
  *   Mail addressed to Michael T. Pace and associated delivery locations, including PO Box 1033 (Lanesborough, MA) and 37 Corey Road, Hancock, Massachusetts 01237-1033.

The FOIA is about records we have in our possession. The FOIA provides only for disclosing federal agency records and does not require government agencies to answer questions, conduct unreasonably burdensome research, or create records[1]. FOIA does not let you appeal any investigative decisions or ask us to research aside from searching for responsive records. We advise requestors to carefully review the documents we release as they may, in what they say or fail to say, answer requestor questions.

The FOIA sheds light on an agency's performance of its statutory duties. The FOIA provides that any person has a right, enforceable in court, to obtain access to federal agency records, unless such records (or portions of them) are protected from public disclosure by one of nine exemptions or by one of three special law enforcement record exclusions.


The U.S. Postal Service comprises the Postal Service, the U.S. Postal Inspection Service (USPIS); the Postal Rate Commission (PRC); and the OIG; each with its own area of responsibility and individual FOIA office. This response is on behalf of the OIG only.

The Postal Service Records Office establishes procedures to ensure Postal Service records management practices comply with both the FOIA and Privacy Act. The Records Office is the designated custodian for non-law enforcement Postal Service records, including but not limited to contracting and employment/personnel records.


The USPIS is the law enforcement, crime prevention, and security arm of USPS, and is the designated custodian for postal records relating to Postal Service law enforcement issues. The USPIS is a federal law enforcement entity charged with enforcing the Federal statutes involving mail fraud, illegal drugs, external mail theft, and other postal crimes, including revenue deficiencies, and are responsible for safety and security of all postal employees and facilities. Additional information regarding the USPIS FOIA program can be found at Terms of Service and Legal | USPIS<https://www.uspis.gov/terms-of-service#freedom-of-information-act>.

Additional information regarding the Postal Service and USPIS FOIA programs can be found at Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) - Who We Are - About.usps.com<https://about.usps.com/who/legal/foia/?_gl=1*ls58y6*_ga*MzUzMDM5MzU1LjE3NTE4OTIwNzE.*_ga_QM3XHZ2B95*czE3NzA5MDY3OTckbzckZzAkdDE3NzA5MDY3OTgkajU5JGwwJGgw>.

The PRC is an independent agency with the mission to ensure transparency and accountability of the U.S. Postal Service and foster a vital and efficient universal mail system. The PRC has exercised regulatory oversight over the Postal Service since its creation by the Postal Reorganization Act of 1970, with expanded responsibilities under the Postal Accountability and Enhancement Act of 2006.



The OIG is an independent agency, created in 1997, with oversight authority over audits and investigations of Postal, Inspection Service, and PRC fraud, abuse, and misconduct. The OIG's mission is to prevent, detect, and report fraud, theft, and misconduct, and promote efficiency in the operation of the Postal Service.


While the OIG has "oversight authority" for activities of the above referenced entities, the OIG is a separate agency and does not have management authority over the daily operations of either the Postal Service or USPIS. In addition, the OIG FOIA office does not have access to records maintained by the Postal Service or UPSIS and cannot direct either FOIA office to release records under their control. Likewise, the OIG Deputy General Counsel has no authority to review FOIA determinations issued by the Postal Service or USPIS.

Additional information/documentation is required before we can continue processing your request in this office.

Please provide a full address information, including ZIP code, associated with the Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort and the Lanesborough, Massachusetts post office.  We also require any email addresses or phone numbers used to file your complaints.

Because you are requesting records potentially located under your name, under postal regulations found at 39 C.F.R. § 265.3(d), "First-party requests. A requester who is making a request for records about himself must provide verification of identity sufficient to satisfy the component as to his identity prior to release of the record. For Privacy Act-protected records, the requester must further comply with the procedures set forth in 39 CFR 266.6."

This regulation requires requesters to provide proof of their identity before an agency can begin processing their request. This ensures records containing the requester's name are not accidentally released to the wrong person.

Please complete and return the attached OIG Certification of Identity and Privacy Waiver along with a copy of a valid driver's license, employee identification, passport, or other identification clearly showing your face.

Not completing the certification and proof of identity/privacy waiver process will limit our ability to release any responsive records we find in our files. They may be withheld in full or contain redactions made under FOIA Exemptions 5 U.S.C. § 552 (b)(3), (b)(6), and(b)(7)(c).


Exemption (b)(3) provides agencies may withhold records exempted from disclosure by another statute that "establishes particular criteria for withholding or refers to particular types of matters to be withheld."  Under the Inspector General Act of 1978, an OIG is duty bound to protect information provided by agency employees during an investigation. Public Law 95-452 5 U.S.C. app. 3 § 7(b).



Exemption (b)(6) pertains to information the release of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of the personal privacy of third parties. The withheld material includes names, titles, and identifying information of private citizens. This information is not appropriate for discretionary disclosure.



Exemption (b)(7)(c) permits the withholding of records or information compiled for law enforcement purposes, the release of which could constitute an unwarranted invasion of the personal privacy of third parties. Lacking an individual's consent or an overriding public interest, third party investigatory records and/or allegations of misconduct must be withheld under Exemption (7)(c). This includes the names of, or identifying statements made by, any persons making allegations against the subject of an OIG investigation.


When we receive the signed completed form(s) and appropriate proof of identity, we will search for records responsive to your request. Any responsive records located will be processed under established FOIA guidelines and a final determination will be issued.

Currently, we are placing your request on hold. If we do not receive the requested address and email information along with the signed completed form and proof of identity before COB April 27, we will presume you are no longer interested in pursuing this request and will administratively close your file. You may file another request in the future when you are able to provide the required documentation. You may return the completed form and proof of identity via e-mail at foia@uspsoig.gov<mailto:foia@uspsoig.gov> or U.S. Mail at 1735 N. Lynn Street, Arlington, VA 22209.

Sincerely,

USPS OIG FOIA Team

title: Public Records Request – Escalated Fire and Life Safety Complaint (Obstructed Means of Egress – Jiminy Peak, Hancock, MA)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request – Escalated Fire and Life Safety Complaint (Obstructed Means of Egress – Jiminy Peak, Hancock, MA)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law (M.G.L. c. 66, § 10), I hereby request copies of all records related to a fire and life safety complaint concerning obstructed means of egress at Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort, located in Hancock, Massachusetts.

On or about January 31, 2026, a complaint was submitted regarding items placed within a designated exit path in the interior hallway outside Unit 844. The condition includes a trash receptacle and other objects reducing the clear width of egress, despite posted signage indicating that such items are not permitted in the hallway.

This situation appears to implicate applicable provisions of 527 CMR, NFPA 101, and the Massachusetts State Building Code requiring that means of egress remain clear and unobstructed at all times.

This matter has been raised with local and state-level entities, including the Town of Hancock and the Massachusetts Department of Fire Services, and has also been submitted through public records channels. No confirmation has been received that the condition has been reviewed, inspected, or corrected.

This request includes, but is not limited to:

• Any records received from or shared with the Town of Hancock, Hancock Fire Department, or local code enforcement authorities regarding this complaint  
• Any communications, referrals, or escalations involving the Massachusetts Department of Fire Services or Department of Public Safety and Inspections  
• Any internal or external communications (including emails, memoranda, or notes) referencing this incident or property  
• Any inspection records, reports, or determinations related to Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort concerning means of egress  
• Any records indicating whether this matter has been reviewed, investigated, or remains open  

The timeframe for this request is January 1, 2026 through the present.

If any responsive records are withheld, please provide the specific statutory basis for each denial.

I request that all responsive records be provided in electronic format where available.

I also request a waiver of any fees, as this request concerns fire and life safety conditions affecting building occupants and is made in the public interest.

If this request is more appropriately directed to another office, please forward it accordingly.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Thomas Pace

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law (M.G.L. c. 66, § 10), I hereby request copies of all records related to a fire and life safety complaint submitted on or about January 31, 2026, concerning obstructed means of egress in the interior hallway outside Unit 844 at Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort, located in Hancock, Massachusetts.

This request includes, but is not limited to:

• Any complaint logs, incident reports, or intake records reflecting receipt of the complaint  
• Any internal or external communications (including emails, notes, or memoranda) referencing the complaint  
• Any records reflecting actions taken, inspections conducted, or determinations made  
• Any correspondence between the Town of Hancock (including the Fire Department and/or any municipal office) and Jiminy Peak Mountain Resort regarding this issue  
• Any records indicating whether the complaint was reviewed, investigated, or closed  

The timeframe for this request is January 31, 2026 through the present.

If any responsive records are withheld, please provide the specific statutory basis for each denial.

I request that all responsive records be provided in electronic format where available.

I also request a waiver of any fees, as this request concerns fire and life safety conditions affecting building occupants and is made in the public interest.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your response within 10 business days.

Sincerely,  
Michael T. Pace

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Thomas Pace
